methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: The PrizePool must hold at least as many prize tokens as have been accounted for in the accumulator minus the total withdrawn.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the actual token balance falls below the accounted balance, the PrizePool becomes insolvent, preventing winners from claiming their legitimate prizes.

invariants.description: The liquidator cannot extract more yield than is currently available (excluding the yield fee).
invariants.attack_scenario: A malicious liquidator could drain user principal from the vault by liquidating more shares than the accrued yield allows.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_liquidationLimit(uint256 amountOut, address tokenOut) public view { assert(amountOut <= _liquidatableBalanceOf(tokenOut)); }

invariants.description: The fee paid to the claimer cannot exceed the total prize size for the tier being claimed.
invariants.attack_scenario: A claimer could drain more than the prize size, stealing funds allocated for the reserve or other prize tiers.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_claimFeeBounds(uint96 fee, uint8 tier) public view { assert(fee <= _getTier(tier, numberOfTiers).prizeSize); }

invariants.description: The total supply tracked in the TwabController must exactly equal the sum of all individual user balances for a given vault.
invariants.attack_scenario: A flaw in the mint/burn or transfer logic could allow users to artificially inflate their balance without increasing the total supply, granting them disproportionate odds to win prizes.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_totalSupplyConservation(address vault) public view { assert(totalSupply(vault) == _sumAllUserBalances(vault)); }

invariants.description: A prize claim record can only transition from false to true and must never revert to false.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the claim status can be reset, a malicious actor could claim the exact same prize multiple times, draining the prize pool liquidity.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_claimMonotonicity(address vault, address winner, uint16 drawId, uint8 tier, uint32 prizeIndex) public view { bool current = claimedPrizes[vault][winner][drawId][tier][prizeIndex]; assert(!past_claimed || current); }

invariants.description: The total shares managed by the vault must exactly equal the minted ERC20 supply plus the accrued but unminted yield fees.
invariants.attack_scenario: If yield fee accounting desyncs from actual shares, it could lead to inflation of shares or permanent loss of yield for the protocol.

invariants.description: The global prizeTokenPerShare value must be monotonically increasing.
invariants.attack_scenario: If prizeTokenPerShare decreases, past draws would retroactively increase in size, or remaining liquidity would be miscalculated, leading to systemic insolvency.

invariants.description: The total delegated supply must equal the sum of all individual delegate balances (excluding the SPONSORSHIP_ADDRESS).
invariants.attack_scenario: If delegate balances exceed the total delegated supply, users could have higher winning odds than the system accounts for, diluting legitimate users' chances.
